NKJV.1: Now it came to pass, when Jesus had finished these sayings, that He departed from Galilee and came to the region of Judea beyond the Jordan.
NKJV.2: And great multitudes followed Him, and He healed them there.
NKJV.3: The Pharisees also came to Him, testing Him, and saying to Him, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
NKJV.4: And He answered and said to them, “Have you not read that He who made them at the beginning ‘made them male and female,’
NKJV.5: and said,  ‘For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and  the two shall become one flesh’?
NKJV.6: So then, they are no longer two but one flesh. Therefore what God has joined together, let not man separate.”
NKJV.7: They said to Him, “Why then did Moses command to give a certificate of divorce, and to put her away?”
NKJV.8: He said to them, “Moses, because of the  hardness of your hearts, permitted you to divorce your  wives, but from the beginning it was not so.
NKJV.9: And I say to you, whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another, commits adultery; and whoever marries her who is divorced commits adultery.”
NKJV.10: His disciples said to Him, “If such is the case of the man with his wife, it is better not to marry.”
NKJV.11: But He said to them, “All cannot accept this saying, but only those to whom it has been given:
NKJV.12: For there are eunuchs who were born thus from their mother’s womb, and there are eunuchs who were made eunuchs by men, and there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs for the kingdom of heaven’s sake. He who is able to accept it, let him accept it.”
NKJV.13: Then little children were brought to Him that He might put His hands on them and pray, but the disciples rebuked them.
NKJV.14: But Jesus said, “Let the little children come to Me, and do not forbid them; for  of such is the kingdom of heaven.”
NKJV.15: And He laid His hands on them and departed from there.
NKJV.16: Now behold, one came and said to Him, “Good Teacher, what good thing shall I do that I may have eternal life?”
NKJV.17: So He said to him, “Why do you call Me good? No one is good but One, that is, God. But if you want to enter into life, keep the commandments.”
NKJV.18: He said to Him, “Which ones?” Jesus said, “‘You shall not murder,’ ‘You shall not commit adultery,’ ‘You shall not steal,’ ‘You shall not bear false witness,’
NKJV.19: ‘Honor your father and your mother,’ and, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’ ”
NKJV.20: The young man said to Him, “All these things I have kept from my youth. What do I still lack?”
NKJV.21: Jesus said to him, “If you want to be perfect,  go, sell what you have and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow Me.”
NKJV.22: But when the young man heard that saying, he went away sorrowful, for he had great possessions.
NKJV.23: Then Jesus said to His disciples, “Assuredly, I say to you that  it is hard for a rich man to enter the kingdom of heaven.
NKJV.24: And again I say to you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.”
NKJV.25: When His disciples heard it, they were greatly astonished, saying, “Who then can be saved?”
NKJV.26: But Jesus looked at them and said to them, “With men this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.”
NKJV.27: Then Peter answered and said to Him, “See, we have left all and followed You. Therefore what shall we have?”
NKJV.28: So Jesus said to them, “Assuredly I say to you, that in the regeneration, when the Son of Man sits on the throne of His glory,  you who have followed Me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
NKJV.29: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or wife or children or lands, for My name’s sake, shall receive a hundredfold, and inherit eternal life.
NKJV.30: But many who are first will be last, and the last first.

ESV.1: Now when Jesus had finished these sayings, he went away from Galilee and entered the region of Judea beyond the Jordan.
ESV.2: And large crowds followed him, and he healed them there.
ESV.3: And Pharisees came up to him and tested him by asking, “Is it lawful to divorce one's wife for any cause?”
ESV.4: He answered,  “Have you not read that he who created them from the beginning made them male and female,
ESV.5: and said, ‘Therefore a man shall leave his father and his mother and hold fast to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh’?
ESV.6: So they are no longer two but one flesh. What therefore God has joined together, let not man separate.”
ESV.7: They said to him, “Why then did Moses command one to give a certificate of divorce and to send her away?”
ESV.8: He said to them, “Because of your hardness of heart Moses allowed you to divorce your wives, but from the beginning it was not so.
ESV.9: And I say to you: whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another, commits adultery.”
ESV.10: The disciples said to him, “If such is the case of a man with his wife, it is better not to marry.”
ESV.11: But he said to them,  “Not everyone can receive this saying, but only those to whom it is given.
ESV.12: For there are eunuchs who have been so from birth, and there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by men, and there are eunuchs who have made themselves eunuchs for the sake of the kingdom of heaven. Let the one who is able to receive this receive it.”
ESV.13: Then children were brought to him that he might lay his hands on them and pray. The disciples rebuked the people,
ESV.14: but Jesus said,  “Let the little children come to me and do not hinder them, for to such belongs the kingdom of heaven.”
ESV.15: And he laid his hands on them and went away.
ESV.16: And behold, a man came up to him, saying, “Teacher, what good deed must I do to have eternal life?”
ESV.17: And he said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? There is only one who is good. If you would enter life, keep the commandments.”
ESV.18: He said to him, “Which ones?” And Jesus said,  “You shall not murder, You shall not commit adultery, You shall not steal, You shall not bear false witness,
ESV.19: Honor your father and mother, and, You shall love your neighbor as yourself.”
ESV.20: The young man said to him, “All these I have kept. What do I still lack?”
ESV.21: Jesus said to him, “If you would be perfect, go, sell what you possess and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me.”
ESV.22: When the young man heard this he went away sorrowful, for he had great possessions.
ESV.23: And Jesus said to his disciples, “Truly, I say to you, only with difficulty will a rich person enter the kingdom of heaven.
ESV.24: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.”
ESV.25: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly astonished, saying, “Who then can be saved?”
ESV.26: But Jesus looked at them and said,  “With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.”
ESV.27: Then Peter said in reply, “See, we have left everything and followed you. What then will we have?”
ESV.28: Jesus said to them, “Truly, I say to you, in the new world,  when the Son of Man will sit on his glorious throne, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
ESV.29: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or lands, for my name's sake, will receive a hundredfold  and will inherit eternal life.
ESV.30: But many who are first will be last, and the last first.

NASB.1: When Jesus had finished these words, He departed from Galilee and came into the region of Judea beyond the Jordan;
NASB.2: and large crowds followed Him, and He healed them there.
NASB.3: [Some] Pharisees came to Jesus, testing Him and asking, 'Is it lawful [for a man] to divorce his wife for any reason at all?'
NASB.4: And He answered and said, 'Have you not read that He who created [them] from the beginning MADE THEM MALE AND FEMALE,
NASB.5: and said, 'FOR THIS REASON A MAN SHALL LEAVE HIS FATHER AND MOTHER AND BE JOINED TO HIS WIFE, AND THE TWO SHALL BECOME ONE FLESH '?
NASB.6: 'So they are no longer two, but one flesh. What therefore God has joined together, let no man separate.'
NASB.7: They said to Him, 'Why then did Moses command to GIVE HER A CERTIFICATE OF DIVORCE AND SEND [her] AWAY?'
NASB.8: He said to them, 'Because of your hardness of heart Moses permitted you to divorce your wives; but from the beginning it has not been this way.
NASB.9: 'And I say to you, whoever divorces his wife, except for immorality, and marries another woman commits adultery.'
NASB.10: The disciples said to Him, 'If the relationship of the man with his wife is like this, it is better not to marry.'
NASB.11: But He said to them, 'Not all men [can] accept this statement, but [only] those to whom it has been given.
NASB.12: 'For there are eunuchs who were born that way from their mother's womb; and there are eunuchs who were made eunuchs by men; and there are [also] eunuchs who made themselves eunuchs for the sake of the kingdom of heaven. He who is able to accept [this], let him accept [it].'
NASB.13: Then [some] children were brought to Him so that He might lay His hands on them and pray; and the disciples rebuked them.
NASB.14: But Jesus said, 'Let the children alone, and do not hinder them from coming to Me; for the kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these.'
NASB.15: After laying His hands on them, He departed from there.
NASB.16: And someone came to Him and said, 'Teacher, what good thing shall I do that I may obtain eternal life?'
NASB.17: And He said to him, 'Why are you asking Me about what is good? There is [only] One who is good; but if you wish to enter into life, keep the commandments.'
NASB.18: [Then] he said to Him, 'Which ones?' And Jesus said, 'YOU SHALL NOT COMMIT MURDER; YOU SHALL NOT COMMIT ADULTERY; YOU SHALL NOT STEAL; YOU SHALL NOT BEAR FALSE WITNESS;
NASB.19: HONOR YOUR FATHER AND MOTHER; and YOU SHALL LOVE YOUR NEIGHBOR AS YOURSELF.'
NASB.20: The young man said to Him, 'All these things I have kept; what am I still lacking?'
NASB.21: Jesus said to him, 'If you wish to be complete, go [and] sell your possessions and give to [the] poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow Me.'
NASB.22: But when the young man heard this statement, he went away grieving; for he was one who owned much property.
NASB.23: And Jesus said to His disciples, 'Truly I say to you, it is hard for a rich man to enter the kingdom of heaven.
NASB.24: 'Again I say to you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.'
NASB.25: When the disciples heard [this], they were very astonished and said, 'Then who can be saved?'
NASB.26: And looking at [them] Jesus said to them, 'With people this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.'
NASB.27: Then Peter said to Him, 'Behold, we have left everything and followed You; what then will there be for us?'
NASB.28: And Jesus said to them, 'Truly I say to you, that you who have followed Me, in the regeneration when the Son of Man will sit on His glorious throne, you also shall sit upon twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
NASB.29: 'And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or farms for My name's sake, will receive many times as much, and will inherit eternal life.
NASB.30: 'But many [who are] first will be last; and [the] last, first.

NLT.1: When Jesus had finished saying these things, he left Galilee and went down to the region of Judea east of the Jordan River.
NLT.2: Large crowds followed him there, and he healed their sick.
NLT.3: Some Pharisees came and tried to trap him with this question: “Should a man be allowed to divorce his wife for just any reason?”
NLT.4: “Haven’t you read the Scriptures?” Jesus replied. “They record that from the beginning ‘God made them male and female.’ ”
NLT.5: And he said, “‘This explains why a man leaves his father and mother and is joined to his wife, and the two are united into one.’
NLT.6: Since they are no longer two but one, let no one split apart what God has joined together.”
NLT.7: “Then why did Moses say in the law that a man could give his wife a written notice of divorce and send her away?” they asked.
NLT.8: Jesus replied, “Moses permitted divorce only as a concession to your hard hearts, but it was not what God had originally intended.
NLT.9: And I tell you this, whoever divorces his wife and marries someone else commits adultery — unless his wife has been unfaithful. ”
NLT.10: Jesus’ disciples then said to him, “If this is the case, it is better not to marry!”
NLT.11: “Not everyone can accept this statement,” Jesus said. “Only those whom God helps.
NLT.12: Some are born as eunuchs, some have been made eunuchs by others, and some choose not to marry for the sake of the Kingdom of Heaven. Let anyone accept this who can.”
NLT.13: One day some parents brought their children to Jesus so he could lay his hands on them and pray for them. But the disciples scolded the parents for bothering him.
NLT.14: But Jesus said, “Let the children come to me. Don’t stop them! For the Kingdom of Heaven belongs to those who are like these children.”
NLT.15: And he placed his hands on their heads and blessed them before he left.
NLT.16: Someone came to Jesus with this question: “Teacher, what good deed must I do to have eternal life?”
NLT.17: “Why ask me about what is good?” Jesus replied. “There is only One who is good. But to answer your question — if you want to receive eternal life, keep the commandments.”
NLT.18: “Which ones?” the man asked. And Jesus replied: “‘You must not murder. You must not commit adultery. You must not steal. You must not testify falsely.
NLT.19: Honor your father and mother. Love your neighbor as yourself.’ ”
NLT.20: “I’ve obeyed all these commandments,” the young man replied. “What else must I do?”
NLT.21: Jesus told him, “If you want to be perfect, go and sell all your possessions and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
NLT.22: But when the young man heard this, he went away sad, for he had many possessions.
NLT.23: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you the truth, it is very hard for a rich person to enter the Kingdom of Heaven.
NLT.24: I’ll say it again — it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the Kingdom of God!”
NLT.25: The disciples were astounded. “Then who in the world can be saved?” they asked.
NLT.26: Jesus looked at them intently and said, “Humanly speaking, it is impossible. But with God everything is possible.”
NLT.27: Then Peter said to him, “We’ve given up everything to follow you. What will we get?”
NLT.28: Jesus replied, “I assure you that when the world is made new and the Son of Man sits upon his glorious throne, you who have been my followers will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
NLT.29: And everyone who has given up houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or property, for my sake, will receive a hundred times as much in return and will inherit eternal life.
NLT.30: But many who are the greatest now will be least important then, and those who seem least important now will be the greatest then.

WEB.1: When Jesus had finished these words, he departed from Galilee, and came into the borders of Judea beyond the Jordan.
WEB.2: Great multitudes followed him, and he healed them there.
WEB.3: Pharisees came to him, testing him, and saying, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any reason?”
WEB.4: He answered, “Haven’t you read that he who made them from the beginning made them male and female,
WEB.5: and said, ‘For this cause a man shall leave his father and mother, and shall join to his wife; and the two shall become one flesh?’
WEB.6: So that they are no more two, but one flesh. What therefore God has joined together, don’t let man tear apart.”
WEB.7: They asked him, “Why then did Moses command us to give her a bill of divorce, and divorce her?”
WEB.8: He said to them, “Moses, because of the hardness of your hearts, allowed you to divorce your wives, but from the beginning it has not been so.
WEB.9: I tell you that whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another, commits adultery; and he who marries her when she is divorced commits adultery.”
WEB.10: His disciples said to him, “If this is the case of the man with his wife, it is not expedient to marry.”
WEB.11: But he said to them, “Not all men can receive this saying, but those to whom it is given.
WEB.12: For there are eunuchs who were born that way from their mother’s womb, and there are eunuchs who were made eunuchs by men; and there are eunuchs who made themselves eunuchs for the Kingdom of Heaven’s sake. He who is able to receive it, let him receive it.”
WEB.13: Then little children were brought to him, that he should lay his hands on them and pray; and the disciples rebuked them.
WEB.14: But Jesus said, “Allow the little children, and don’t forbid them to come to me; for the Kingdom of Heaven belongs to ones like these.”
WEB.15: He laid his hands on them, and departed from there.
WEB.16: Behold, one came to him and said, “Good teacher, what good thing shall I do, that I may have eternal life?”
WEB.17: He said to him, “Why do you call me good?  No one is good but one, that is, God. But if you want to enter into life, keep the commandments.”
WEB.18: He said to him, “Which ones?” Jesus said, “‘You shall not murder.’ ‘You shall not commit adultery.’ ‘You shall not steal.’ ‘You shall not offer false testimony.’
WEB.19: ‘Honor your father and your mother.’  And, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’”
WEB.20: The young man said to him, “All these things I have observed from my youth. What do I still lack?”
WEB.21: Jesus said to him, “If you want to be perfect, go, sell what you have, and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow me.”
WEB.22: But when the young man heard the saying, he went away sad, for he was one who had great possessions.
WEB.23: Jesus said to his disciples, “Most certainly I say to you, a rich man will enter into the Kingdom of Heaven with difficulty.
WEB.24: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through a needle’s eye, than for a rich man to enter into God’s Kingdom.”
WEB.25: When the disciples heard it, they were exceedingly astonished, saying, “Who then can be saved?”
WEB.26: Looking at them, Jesus said, “With men this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.”
WEB.27: Then Peter answered, “Behold, we have left everything, and followed you. What then will we have?”
WEB.28: Jesus said to them, “Most certainly I tell you that you who have followed me, in the regeneration when the Son of Man will sit on the throne of his glory, you also will sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
WEB.29: Everyone who has left houses, or brothers, or sisters, or father, or mother, or wife, or children, or lands, for my name’s sake, will receive one hundred times, and will inherit eternal life.
WEB.30: But many will be last who are first; and first who are last.

CSB17.1: When Jesus had finished saying these things, <sup>ⓖ</sup> he departed from Galilee and went to the region of Judea across the Jordan. <sup>ⓗ</sup>
CSB17.2: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them there. <sup>ⓘ</sup>
CSB17.3: Some Pharisees approached him to test him. They asked, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife on any grounds?” <sup>ⓙ</sup>
CSB17.4: “Haven’t you read,” he replied, “that he who created [1]them in the beginning made them male and female,” [2] ⓚ
CSB17.5: and he also said, “For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two will become one flesh? [3] ⓛ
CSB17.6: So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore, what God has joined together, let no one separate.”
CSB17.7: “Why then,” they asked him, “did Moses command us to give divorce papers <sup>ⓜ</sup> and to send her away?”
CSB17.8: He told them, “Moses permitted you to divorce your wives because of the hardness of your hearts, but it was not like that from the beginning.
CSB17.9: I tell you, whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another commits adultery.” <sup>[4]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup>
CSB17.10: His disciples <sup>ⓐ</sup> said to him, “If the relationship of a man with his wife is like this, it’s better not to marry.”
CSB17.11: He responded, “Not everyone can accept this saying, but only those to whom it has been given. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.12: For there are eunuchs who were born that way from their mother’s womb, there are eunuchs who were made by men, and there are eunuchs who have made themselves that way because of the kingdom of heaven. The one who is able to accept it should accept it.”
CSB17.13: Then children were brought to Jesus for him to place his hands on them and pray, but the disciples rebuked them. <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.14: Jesus said, “Leave the children alone, and don’t try to keep them from coming to me, because the kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these.” <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓓ</sup>
CSB17.15: After placing his hands on them, he went on from there.
CSB17.16: Just then someone came up and asked him, <sup>ⓔ</sup> “Teacher, what good must I do to have eternal life?” <sup>ⓕ</sup>
CSB17.17: “Why do you ask me about what is good?” <sup>[6]</sup> he said to him. “There is only one who is good. <sup>[7]</sup> If you want to enter into life, keep the commandments.” <sup>ⓖ</sup>
CSB17.18: “Which ones?” he asked him. Jesus answered: Do not murder; do not commit adultery; do not steal; do not bear false witness; ⓗ
CSB17.19: honor your father and your mother; and love your neighbor as yourself. [8] ⓘ
CSB17.20: “I have kept all these,” <sup>[9]</sup> the young man told him. “What do I still lack?”
CSB17.21: “If you want to be perfect,” <sup>[10]</sup> Jesus said to him, “go, sell your belongings and give to the poor, <sup>ⓙ</sup>and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
CSB17.22: When the young man heard that, he went away grieving, because he had many possessions.
CSB17.23: Jesus said to his disciples, “Truly I tell you, it will be hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.24: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter the kingdom of God.” <sup>ⓛ</sup>
CSB17.25: When the disciples heard this, they were utterly astonished and asked, “Then who can be saved?”
CSB17.26: Jesus looked at them and said, “With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.” <sup>ⓜ</sup>
CSB17.27: Then Peter responded to him, “See, we have left everything and followed you. So what will there be for us?”
CSB17.28: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, in the renewal of all things, <sup>ⓝ</sup>when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, <sup>ⓐ</sup>you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
CSB17.29: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother <sup>[11]</sup>or children or fields because of my name will receive a hundred times more and will inherit eternal life. <sup>ⓑ</sup>
CSB17.30: But many who are first will be last, and the last first. <sup>ⓒ</sup>

AMP.1: Now when Jesus had finished saying these things, He left Galilee and went into the part of Judea that is beyond the Jordan;
AMP.2: and large crowds followed Him, and He healed them there.
AMP.3: And Pharisees came to Jesus, testing Him and asking, "Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for just any reason?"
AMP.4: He replied, "Have you never read that He who created them from the beginning <e>made them male and female</e>,
AMP.5: and said, '<e>For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and shall be joined inseparably to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh</e>'?
AMP.6: So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore, what God has joined together, let no one separate."
AMP.7: The Pharisees said to Him, "Why then did Moses command us to <e>give her a certificate of divorce and send her away</e>?"
AMP.8: He said to them, "Because your hearts were hard and stubborn Moses permitted you to divorce your wives; but from the beginning it has not been this way.
AMP.9: I say to you, whoever divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, and marries another woman commits adultery."
AMP.10: The disciples said to Jesus, "If the relationship of a man with his wife is like this, it is better not to marry."
AMP.11: But He said to them, "Not all men can accept this statement, but only those to whom [the capacity to receive] it has been given.
AMP.12: For there are eunuchs who have been born that way from their mother's womb [making them incapable of consummating a marriage]; and there are eunuchs who have been made eunuchs by men [for royal service]; and there are eunuchs who have made themselves so for the sake of the kingdom of heaven. He who is able to accept this, let him accept it."
AMP.13: Then children were brought to Jesus so that He might place His hands on them [for a blessing] and pray; but the disciples reprimanded them.
AMP.14: But He said, "Leave the children alone, and do not forbid them from coming to Me; for the kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these."
AMP.15: After placing His hands on them [for a blessing], He went on from there.
AMP.16: And someone came to Him and said, "Teacher, what [essentially] good thing shall I do to obtain eternal life [that is, eternal salvation in the Messiah's kingdom]?"
AMP.17: Jesus answered, "Why are you asking Me about what is [essentially] good? There is only One who is [essentially] good; but if you wish to enter into eternal life, keep the commandments."
AMP.18: He said to Jesus, "Which commandments?" And Jesus answered, "<e>You shall not commit murder; You shall not commit adultery; You shall not steal; You shall not give false testimony</e>;
AMP.19: Honor your father and mother; and love your neighbor as yourself" [that is, unselfishly seek the best or higher good for others].
AMP.20: The young man said to Him, "I have kept all these things [from my youth]; what do I still lack?"
AMP.21: Jesus answered him, "If you wish to be perfect [that is, have the spiritual maturity that accompanies godly character with no moral or ethical deficiencies], go and sell what you have and give [the money] to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; and come, follow Me [becoming My disciple, believing and trusting in Me and walking the same path of life that I walk]."
AMP.22: But when the young man heard this, he left grieving and distressed, for he owned much property and had many possessions [which he treasured more than his relationship with God].
AMP.23: Jesus said to His disciples, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, it is difficult for a rich man [who clings to possessions and status as security] to enter the kingdom of heaven.
AMP.24: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle, than for a rich man [who places his faith in wealth and status] to enter the kingdom of God."
AMP.25: When the disciples heard this, they were completely astonished and bewildered, saying, "Then who can be saved [from the wrath of God]?"
AMP.26: But Jesus looked at them and said, "With people [as far as it depends on them] it is impossible, but with God all things are possible."
AMP.27: Then Peter answered Him, saying, "Look, we have given up everything and followed You [becoming Your disciples and accepting You as Teacher and Lord]; what then will there be for us?"
AMP.28: Jesus said to them, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, in the renewal [that is, the Messianic restoration and regeneration of all things] when the Son of Man sits on His glorious throne, you [who have followed Me, becoming My disciples] will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
AMP.29: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or farms for My name's sake will receive many times as much, and will inherit eternal life.
AMP.30: But many who are first [in this world] will be last [in the world to come]; and the last, first.

MSG.1: When Jesus had completed these teachings, he left Galilee and crossed the region of Judea on the other side of the Jordan.
MSG.2: Great crowds followed him there, and he healed them.
MSG.3: One day the Pharisees were badgering him: "Is it legal for a man to divorce his wife for any reason?"
MSG.4: He answered, "Haven't you read in your Bible that the Creator originally made man and woman for each other, male and female?
MSG.5: And because of this, a man leaves father and mother and is firmly bonded to his wife, becoming one flesh—no longer two bodies but one.
MSG.6: Because God created this organic union of the two sexes, no one should desecrate his art by cutting them apart."
MSG.7: They shot back in rebuttal, "If that's so, why did Moses give instructions for divorce papers and divorce procedures?"
MSG.8: Jesus said, "Moses provided for divorce as a concession to your hardheartedness, but it is not part of God's original plan.
MSG.9: I'm holding you to the original plan, and holding you liable for adultery if you divorce your faithful wife and then marry someone else. I make an exception in cases where the spouse has committed adultery."
MSG.10: Jesus' disciples objected, "If those are the terms of marriage, we're stuck. Why get married?"
MSG.11: But Jesus said, "Not everyone is mature enough to live a married life. It requires a certain aptitude and grace. Marriage isn't for everyone.
MSG.12: Some, from birth seemingly, never give marriage a thought. Others never get asked—or accepted. And some decide not to get married for kingdom reasons. But if you're capable of growing into the largeness of marriage, do it."
MSG.13: One day children were brought to Jesus in the hope that he would lay hands on them and pray over them. The disciples shooed them off.
MSG.14: But Jesus intervened: "Let the children alone, don't prevent them from coming to me. God's kingdom is made up of people like these."
MSG.15: After laying hands on them, he left.
MSG.16: Another day, a man stopped Jesus and asked, "Teacher, what good thing must I do to get eternal life?"
MSG.17: Jesus said, "Why do you question me about what's good? God is the One who is good. If you want to enter the life of God, just do what he tells you."
MSG.18: The man asked, "What in particular?" Jesus said, "Don't murder, don't commit adultery, don't steal, don't lie,
MSG.19: honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as you do yourself."
MSG.20: The young man said, "I've done all that. What's left?"
MSG.21: "If you want to give it all you've got," Jesus replied, "go sell your possessions; give everything to the poor. All your wealth will then be in heaven. Then come follow me."
MSG.22: That was the last thing the young man expected to hear. And so, crestfallen, he walked away. He was holding on tight to a lot of things, and he couldn't bear to let go.
MSG.23: As he watched him go, Jesus told his disciples, "Do you have any idea how difficult it is for the rich to enter God's kingdom?
MSG.24: Let me tell you, it's easier to gallop a camel through a needle's eye than for the rich to enter God's kingdom."
MSG.25: The disciples were staggered. "Then who has any chance at all?"
MSG.26: Jesus looked hard at them and said, "No chance at all if you think you can pull it off yourself. Every chance in the world if you trust God to do it."
MSG.27: Then Peter chimed in, "We left everything and followed you. What do we get out of it?"
MSG.28: Jesus replied, "Yes, you have followed me. In the re-creation of the world, when the Son of Man will rule gloriously, you who have followed me will also rule, starting with the twelve tribes of Israel.
MSG.29: And not only you, but anyone who sacrifices home, family, fields—whatever—because of me will get it all back a hundred times over, not to mention the considerable bonus of eternal life.
MSG.30: This is the Great Reversal: many of the first ending up last, and the last first.

NIV.1:
Divorce
When Jesus had finished saying these things, he left Galilee and went into the region of Judea to the other side of the Jordan.
NIV.2: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them there.
NIV.3: Some Pharisees came to him to test him. They asked, “Is it lawful for a man to divorce his wife for any and every reason?”
NIV.4: “Haven’t you read,” he replied, “that at the beginning the Creator ‘made them male and female,’
NIV.5: and said, ‘For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and be united to his wife, and the two will become one flesh’?
NIV.6: So they are no longer two, but one. Therefore what God has joined together, let man not separate.”
NIV.7: “Why then,” they asked, “did Moses command that a man give his wife a certificate of divorce and send her away?”
NIV.8: Jesus replied, “Moses permitted you to divorce your wives because your hearts were hard. But it was not this way from the beginning.
NIV.9: I tell you that anyone who divorces his wife, except for marital unfaithfulness, and marries another woman commits adultery.”
NIV.10: The disciples said to him, “If this is the situation between a husband and wife, it is better not to marry.”
NIV.11: Jesus replied, “Not everyone can accept this word, but only those to whom it has been given.
NIV.12: For some are eunuchs because they were born that way; others were made that way by men; and others have renounced marriage because of the kingdom of heaven. The one who can accept this should accept it.”
NIV.13:
The Little Children and Jesus
Then little children were brought to Jesus for him to place his hands on them and pray for them. But the disciples rebuked those who brought them.
NIV.14: Jesus said, “Let the little children come to me, and do not hinder them, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these.”
NIV.15: When he had placed his hands on them, he went on from there.
NIV.16:
The Rich Young Man
Now a man came up to Jesus and asked, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to get eternal life?”
NIV.17: “Why do you ask me about what is good?” Jesus replied. “There is only One who is good. If you want to enter life, obey the commandments.”
NIV.18:
“Which ones?” the man inquired.
Jesus replied, “‘Do not murder, do not commit adultery, do not steal, do not give false testimony,
NIV.19: honor your father and mother,’ and ‘love your neighbor as yourself.’”
NIV.20: “All these I have kept,” the young man said. “What do I still lack?”
NIV.21: Jesus answered, “If you want to be perfect, go, sell your possessions and give to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
NIV.22: When the young man heard this, he went away sad, because he had great wealth.
NIV.23: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you the truth, it is hard for a rich man to enter the kingdom of heaven.
NIV.24: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich man to enter the kingdom of God.”
NIV.25: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly astonished and asked, “Who then can be saved?”
NIV.26: Jesus looked at them and said, “With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.”
NIV.27: Peter answered him, “We have left everything to follow you! What then will there be for us?”
NIV.28: Jesus said to them, “I tell you the truth, at the renewal of all things, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
NIV.29: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or fields for my sake will receive a hundred times as much and will inherit eternal life.
NIV.30: But many who are first will be last, and many who are last will be first.

NRSVCE.2: Large crowds followed him, and he cured them there.

NRSVCE.4: He answered, “Have you not read that the one who made them at the beginning ‘made them male and female,’
NRSVCE.5: and said, ‘For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife, and the two shall become one flesh’?
NRSVCE.6: So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate.”
NRSVCE.7: They said to him, “Why then did Moses command us to give a certificate of dismissal and to divorce her?”
NRSVCE.8: He said to them, “It was because you were so hard-hearted that Moses allowed you to divorce your wives, but from the beginning it was not so.
NRSVCE.9: And I say to you, whoever divorces his wife, except for unchastity, and marries another commits adultery.”

NRSVCE.17: And he said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? There is only one who is good. If you wish to enter into life, keep the commandments.”
NRSVCE.18: He said to him, “Which ones?” And Jesus said, “You shall not murder; You shall not commit adultery; You shall not steal; You shall not bear false witness;

NRSVCE.21: Jesus said to him, “If you wish to be perfect, go, sell your possessions, and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven; then come, follow me.”
NRSVCE.22: When the young man heard this word, he went away grieving, for he had many possessions.
NRSVCE.23: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “Truly I tell you, it will be hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven.
NRSVCE.24: Again I tell you, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for someone who is rich to enter the kingdom of God.”
NRSVCE.25: When the disciples heard this, they were greatly astounded and said, “Then who can be saved?”
NRSVCE.26: But Jesus looked at them and said, “For mortals it is impossible, but for God all things are possible.”
NRSVCE.27: Then Peter said in reply, “Look, we have left everything and followed you. What then will we have?”
NRSVCE.28: Jesus said to them, “Truly I tell you, at the renewal of all things, when the Son of Man is seated on the throne of his glory, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
NRSVCE.29: And everyone who has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or fields, for my name’s sake, will receive a hundredfold, and will inherit eternal life.
NRSVCE.30: But many who are first will be last, and the last will be first.

NET.1: Now when Jesus finished these sayings, he left Galilee and went to the region of Judea beyond the Jordan River.
NET.2: Large crowds followed him, and he healed them there.
NET.3: Then some Pharisees came to him in order to test him. They asked, “Is it lawful to divorce a wife for any cause?”
NET.4: He answered, “Have you not read that from the beginning the Creator made them male and female,
NET.5: and said, ‘For this reason a man will leave his father and mother and will be united with his wife, and the two will become one flesh’?
NET.6: So they are no longer two, but one flesh. Therefore what God has joined together, let no one separate.”
NET.7: They said to him, “Why then did Moses command us to give a certificate of dismissal and to divorce her?”
NET.8: Jesus said to them, “Moses permitted you to divorce your wives because of your hard hearts, but from the beginning it was not this way.
NET.9: Now I say to you that whoever divorces his wife, except for immorality, and marries another commits adultery.”
NET.10: The disciples said to him, “If this is the case of a husband with a wife, it is better not to marry!”
NET.11: He said to them, “Not everyone can accept this statement, except those to whom it has been given.
NET.12: For there are some eunuchs who were that way from birth, and some who were made eunuchs by others, and some who became eunuchs for the sake of the kingdom of heaven. The one who is able to accept this should accept it.”
NET.13: Then little children were brought to him for him to lay his hands on them and pray. But the disciples scolded those who brought them.
NET.14: But Jesus said, “Let the little children come to me and do not try to stop them, for the kingdom of heaven belongs to such as these.”
NET.15: And he placed his hands on them and went on his way.
NET.16: Now someone came up to him and said, “Teacher, what good thing must I do to gain eternal life?”
NET.17: He said to him, “Why do you ask me about what is good? There is only one who is good. But if you want to enter into life, keep the commandments.”
NET.18: “Which ones?” he asked. Jesus replied, “Do not murder, do not commit adultery, do not steal, do not give false testimony,
NET.19: honor your father and mother, and love your neighbor as yourself.”
NET.20: The young man said to him, “I have wholeheartedly obeyed all these laws. What do I still lack?”
NET.21: Jesus said to him, “If you wish to be perfect, go sell your possessions and give the money to the poor, and you will have treasure in heaven. Then come, follow me.”
NET.22: But when the young man heard this he went away sorrowful, for he was very rich.
NET.23: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “I tell you the truth, it will be hard for a rich person to enter the kingdom of heaven!
NET.24: Again I say, it is easier for a camel to go through the eye of a needle than for a rich person to enter into the kingdom of God.”
NET.25: The disciples were greatly astonished when they heard this and said, “Then who can be saved?”
NET.26: Jesus looked at them and replied, “This is impossible for mere humans, but for God all things are possible.”
NET.27: Then Peter said to him, “Look, we have left everything to follow you! What then will there be for us?”
NET.28: Jesus said to them, “I tell you the truth: In the age when all things are renewed, when the Son of Man sits on his glorious throne, you who have followed me will also sit on twelve thrones, judging the twelve tribes of Israel.
NET.29: And whoever has left houses or brothers or sisters or father or mother or children or fields for my sake will receive a hundred times as much and will inherit eternal life.
NET.30: But many who are first will be last, and the last first.
